21: // setupEnv parses args and may replace them and sets some env vars to known
22: // values based on format options
23: func setupEnv(args []string) (retArgs []string, format string, outputCurlString bool) {
24: 	var nextArgFormat bool
25: 
26: 	for _, arg := range args {
27: 		if nextArgFormat {
28: 			nextArgFormat = false
29: 			format = arg
30: 			continue
31: 		}
32: 
33: 		if arg == "--" {
34: 			break
35: 		}
36: 
37: 		if len(args) == 1 && arg == "-version" {
38: 			args = []string{"version"}
39: 			break
40: 		}
41: 
42: 		if arg == "-output-curl-string" {
43: 			outputCurlString = true
44: 			continue
45: 		}
46: 
47: 		// Parse a given flag here, which overrides the env var
48: 		if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "-format=") {
49: 			format = strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "-format=")
50: 		}
51: 		// Handle the case where it is specified without an equal sign
52: 		if arg == "-format" {
53: 			nextArgFormat = true
54: 		}
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	envBoundaryCLIFormat := os.Getenv(base.EnvBoundaryCLIFormat)
58: 	// If we did not parse a value, fetch the env var
59: 	if format == "" && envBoundaryCLIFormat != "" {
60: 		format = envBoundaryCLIFormat
61: 	}
62: 	// Lowercase for consistency
63: 	format = strings.ToLower(format)
64: 	if format == "" {
65: 		format = "table"
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	return args, format, outputCurlString
69: }

72: 	Stdout  io.Writer
73: 	Stderr  io.Writer

75: }

77: func Run(args []string) int {
78: 	return RunCustom(args, nil)
79: }

81: // RunCustom allows passing in a base command template to pass to other
82: // commands. Currently, this is only used for setting a custom token helper.
83: func RunCustom(args []string, runOpts *RunOptions) int {
84: 	if runOpts == nil {
85: 		runOpts = &RunOptions{}
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	var format string
89: 	var outputCurlString bool
90: 	args, format, outputCurlString = setupEnv(args)
91: 
92: 	// Don't use color if disabled
93: 	useColor := true
94: 	if os.Getenv(base.EnvBoundaryCLINoColor) != "" || color.NoColor {
95: 		useColor = false
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	if runOpts.Stdout == nil {
99: 		runOpts.Stdout = os.Stdout
100: 	}
101: 	if runOpts.Stderr == nil {
102: 		runOpts.Stderr = os.Stderr
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	// Only use colored UI if stdout is a tty, and not disabled
106: 	if useColor && format == "table" {
107: 		if f, ok := runOpts.Stdout.(*os.File); ok {
108: 			runOpts.Stdout = colorable.NewColorable(f)
109: 		}
110: 		if f, ok := runOpts.Stderr.(*os.File); ok {
111: 			runOpts.Stderr = colorable.NewColorable(f)
112: 		}
113: 	} else {
114: 		runOpts.Stdout = colorable.NewNonColorable(runOpts.Stdout)
115: 		runOpts.Stderr = colorable.NewNonColorable(runOpts.Stderr)
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	uiErrWriter := runOpts.Stderr
119: 	if outputCurlString {
120: 		uiErrWriter = ioutil.Discard
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	ui := &base.BoundaryUI{
124: 		Ui: &cli.ColoredUi{
125: 			ErrorColor: cli.UiColorRed,
126: 			WarnColor:  cli.UiColorYellow,
127: 			Ui: &cli.BasicUi{
128: 				Reader:      bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin),
129: 				Writer:      runOpts.Stdout,
130: 				ErrorWriter: uiErrWriter,
131: 			},
132: 		},
133: 		Format: format,
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	serverCmdUi := &base.BoundaryUI{
137: 		Ui: &cli.ColoredUi{
138: 			ErrorColor: cli.UiColorRed,
139: 			WarnColor:  cli.UiColorYellow,
140: 			Ui: &cli.BasicUi{
141: 				Reader: bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin),
142: 				Writer: runOpts.Stdout,
143: 			},
144: 		},
145: 		Format: format,
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	switch format {
149: 	case "table", "json":
150: 	default:
151: 		ui.Error(fmt.Sprintf("Invalid output format: %s", format))
152: 		return 1
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	initCommands(ui, serverCmdUi, runOpts)
156: 
157: 	hiddenCommands := []string{"version"}
158: 
159: 	cli := &cli.CLI{
160: 		Name:     "boundary",
161: 		Args:     args,
162: 		Commands: Commands,
163: 		HelpFunc: groupedHelpFunc(
164: 			cli.BasicHelpFunc("boundary"),
165: 		),
166: 		HelpWriter:                 runOpts.Stderr,
167: 		HiddenCommands:             hiddenCommands,
168: 		Autocomplete:               true,
169: 		AutocompleteNoDefaultFlags: true,
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	exitCode, err := cli.Run()
173: 	if outputCurlString {
174: 		if exitCode == 0 {
175: 			fmt.Fprint(runOpts.Stderr, "Could not generate cURL command\n")
176: 			return 1
177: 		} else {
178: 			if api.LastOutputStringError == nil {
179: 				if exitCode == 127 {
180: 					// Usage, just pass it through
181: 					return exitCode
182: 				}
183: 				fmt.Fprint(runOpts.Stderr, "cURL command not set by API operation; run without -output-curl-string to see the generated error\n")
184: 				return exitCode
185: 			}
186: 			if !strings.Contains(api.LastOutputStringError.Error(), api.ErrOutputStringRequest) {
187: 				runOpts.Stdout.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf("Error creating request string: %s\n", api.LastOutputStringError.Error())))
188: 				return 1
189: 			}
190: 			runOpts.Stdout.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n", api.LastOutputStringError.CurlString())))
191: 			return 0
192: 		}
193: 	} else if err != nil {
194: 		fmt.Fprintf(runOpts.Stderr, "Error executing CLI: %s\n", err.Error())
195: 		return 1
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	return exitCode
199: }

201: func groupedHelpFunc(f cli.HelpFunc) cli.HelpFunc {
202: 	return func(commands map[string]cli.CommandFactory) string {
203: 		var b bytes.Buffer
204: 		tw := tabwriter.NewWriter(&b, 0, 2, 6, ' ', 0)
205: 
206: 		fmt.Fprintf(tw, "Usage: boundary <command> [args]\n")
207: 
208: 		otherCommands := make([]string, 0, len(commands))
209: 		for k := range commands {
210: 			otherCommands = append(otherCommands, k)
211: 		}
212: 		sort.Strings(otherCommands)
213: 
214: 		fmt.Fprintf(tw, "\n")
215: 		fmt.Fprintf(tw, "Commands:\n")
216: 		for _, v := range otherCommands {
217: 			printCommand(tw, v, commands[v])
218: 		}
219: 
220: 		tw.Flush()
221: 
222: 		return strings.TrimSpace(b.String())
223: 	}
224: }
225: 
226: func printCommand(w io.Writer, name string, cmdFn cli.CommandFactory) {
227: 	cmd, err := cmdFn()
228: 	if err != nil {
229: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("failed to load %q command: %s", name, err))
230: 	}
231: 	fmt.Fprintf(w, "    %s\t%s\n", name, cmd.Synopsis())
232: }

748: // reloading. This channel will send a message for every

750: func MakeSighupCh() chan struct{} {
751: 	resultCh := make(chan struct{})
752: 
753: 	signalCh := make(chan os.Signal, 4)
754: 	signal.Notify(signalCh, syscall.SIGHUP)
755: 	go func() {
756: 		for {
757: 			<-signalCh
758: 			resultCh <- struct{}{}
759: 		}
760: 	}()
761: 	return resultCh

part.text:
---
## Goal
- Complete rename of authorize-related identifiers to authorize-session across all CLI commands, handler logic, and help text where applicable

## Constraints & Preferences
- Task must be complete when change is applied everywhere and project still typechecks
- Authorize action constant (internal/types/action) should NOT be renamed as it's an IAM action type

## Progress
### Done
- cli/commands.go: Registered "targets authorize-session" command (line 624)
- targets service proto: RPC method `AuthorizeSession` in place (short form `:authorize` exists)
- proxy/proxy.go: Updated `.Authorize()` API call → `.AuthorizeSession()` (line 441)

### In Progress
- targets/target.go flagsMap still uses "authorize" key instead of "authorize-session" (line 42)
- targets/target.go Execute switch cases use string literal "authorize" (lines 240, 285)
- targets/target.go help text references "targets authorize" command format (lines 121-130)
- proxy/proxy.go Help() still contains "Authorize a session..." strings for all proxy variants

### Blocked
- "(none)"

## Key Decisions
- Authorize action constant remains unchanged (internal/types/action/action.go line 17 - distinct IAM action type)
- Only CLI command identifiers and handlers should use authorize-session naming convention
- Proto RPC methods may keep short form `:authorize` for brevity while maintaining full method names

## Next Steps
1. Update targets/target.go flagsMap: Change `"authorize": {"id", "host-id"}` → `"authorize-session": {"id", "host-id"}` (line 42)
2. Update targets/target.go Execute switch statement cases from `"authorize"` to `"authorize-session"` (lines 240, 285)
3. Update proxy/proxy.go Help() strings replacing "Authorize a session..." with "AuthorizeSession a session..." (lines 108-116)
4. Update targets/target.go help text section from "targets authorize" to "targets authorize-session" (lines 123, 127)

## Critical Context
- internal/cmd/commands/targets/target.go line 42 flagsMap uses "authorize" key, needs updating to match CLI registration name "targets authorize-session"
- internal/cmd/commands/targets/target.go lines 240, 285 Execute switch cases use `"authorize"` string literal
- internal/cmd/commands/proxy/proxy.go lines 108-116 Help() output contains "Authorize a session..." variants for TCP, HTTP, SSH, PostgreSQL, RDP commands
- Line 286 Execute function calls `.AuthorizeSession()` - rename may be partially complete but case string literals not updated

## Relevant Files
